enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his DUI plea deal in Denver — a $35 fine, 10 hours community service, and unsupervised probation requiring him to quit weed for four months — then discusses his obsessive work on a website project called Saint Dominium, which maps every Roman battle and town over a thousand years, and closes by acknowledging he's about $5,800 in debt and needs to sell his car and find income.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Was the right call — cheaper and faster than fighting it, and he can still be a marijuana activist through YouTube and his website
Doesn't believe heavy users are truly addicted; plans to prove it by quitting four months as required, then resuming
Sees a break as potentially beneficial — resets tolerance so you can get more stoned when you restart
Doesn't need to fight in court to be a marijuana activist; YouTube and his website are sufficient platforms
Deeply obsessed and motivated by it despite it being a money sink; wants to see how closely he can mirror Rome in a local context

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to Denver last Thursday to handle a plea deal.

claims.subject: plea deal
claims.claim: The plea deal involved a $35 fine and 10 hours of community service.

claims.subject: plea deal
claims.claim: The plea deal requires me to quit smoking weed during probation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took the plea deal because it was less expensive than going to court.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't need to go to court to be a marijuana activist; I can do it through my YouTube and website.

claims.subject: DUI
claims.claim: I can use my DUI as motivation to create content for others in similar situations.

claims.subject: Saint Dominium
claims.claim: I'm creating lists of every battle Rome was involved in from 753 BC to 211 AD.

claims.subject: Saint Dominium
claims.claim: I'm mapping the distance of each battle from Rome, including round trips.

claims.subject: Saint Dominium
claims.claim: I'm listing every town in northern Italy and the Roman Empire with their distances from Rome.

claims.subject: Saint Dominium
claims.claim: I plan to create sister cities and sister battles based on their distances from Rome.

claims.subject: Saint Dominium
claims.claim: I'm dividing the battles into different wars and tribes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been obsessed with working on Saint Dominium for the last couple of weeks.

claims.subject: court appearance
claims.claim: I forgot to sign in when I first arrived at court, so I had to wait for two hours.

claims.subject: court appearance
claims.claim: I watched other people handle their cases while waiting.

claims.subject: prosecutor
claims.claim: The prosecutor asked if I was going to take the plea deal.

claims.subject: probation
claims.claim: The prosecutor mentioned unsupervised probation if the supervising lady didn't show up.

claims.subject: probation
claims.claim: I agreed to unsupervised probation, which doesn't require blood or urine tests.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I told the judge I was guilty of driving while ability impaired, even though I don't believe it's true.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I paid a $35 fine and signed up for community service.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm $1,200 in credit card debt and $4,600 in personal debt.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to get a job or find a way to make money soon.

claims.subject: car
claims.claim: I need to replace my car battery because it died from sitting unused.

claims.subject: car
claims.claim: I plan to sell my car to help get out of debt.

claims.subject: website
claims.claim: I'm going to work on a fundraising page on my website to ask for donations.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: I might start making videos again in the winter to earn money.

claims.subject: Saint Dominium
claims.claim: I'm excited about creating a mirrored Roman kingdom on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently focused on finishing the Saint Dominium page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tend to get hyper-focused on new projects for about three days before burning out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm eating a Subway sandwich now.
